title: implement reverse reader for log reads
body:
in cases where the log file exceeds the available memory of a system, we had a bug that triggered an oom because the entire logfile was being read when the tail parameter was given.  this reads in chunks and is more or less memory safe.

fixes: #5131

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

> Does this do anything for when I want to read an entire log? 

Or tailing more lines than it fits on the memory. I haven't tried it yet but if I understood the code correctly, it does seem to work under the assumption that tailed lines do fit on the memory.

> @yangm97 PTAL
> LGTM
> Does this do anything for when I want to read an entire log? Do we buffer that up now?

reading the entire log is done differently.  not applicable
